stories.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies needed medical attention after being taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match against Ross Smith in Blackpool on Wednesday. While leading the game 2-1, Scotland's Menzies stepped away from the oche and appeared unsteady on his feet as England's Smith and the referee guided him safely off stage. It was later confirmed that Menzies was cleared by medics backstage but was unable to return to the stage to continue, and so forfeited the match. The PDC said: "Cameron experienced high blood pressure, which caused him to feel dizzy and subsequently faint. "Following an assessment by the on-site medical team, Cameron was deemed well enough to return to his accommodation.

stories.title: EIntracht Frankfurt stellt neues Auswärtstrikot mit Retro-Adler vor
stories.summary: Die Eintracht ist retro - zumindest in Sachen Auswärtstrikot. So kehren die Frankfurter zu einem Logo zurück, das sie letztmals 1999 verwendet hatten. Und das viele Fans an eine lokale Biermarke erinnert. Schon seit einigen Tagen geisterten diverse Leaks durch die Sozialen Medien, auch gab die Frankfurter Eintracht selbst kleine Hinweise auf das, was da an diesem Donnerstag um 10 Uhr offiziell vorgestellt wurde: das neue Auswärtstrikot des hessischen Bundesligisten. Weiß mit schwarzen Sponsoren-Streifen auf der Schulter, viel mehr nicht. Kein Firlefanz, keine Spielereien und vor allem eine Replik an vergangene Tage. Denn: Der Retro-Adler ist zurück.

stories.title: Une épidémie de rougeole « hors de contrôle » aux Etats-Unis, sur fond de vaccino-scepticisme d’une partie des habitants et de l’administration Trump
stories.summary: Cet article vous est offert Pour lire gratuitement cet article réservé aux abonnés, connectez-vous Se connecter Vous n'êtes pas inscrit sur Le Monde ? Inscrivez-vous gratuitement Planète Planète Planète Santé mondiale Santé mondiale Santé mondiale Le pays fait face à une accélération du nombre de cas de cette maladie aux conséquences sanitaires potentiellement graves. La baisse de la couverture vaccinale est la principale cause de cette crise. Article réservé aux abonnés Les Etats-Unis font face à une épidémie de rougeole qui ne cesse de prendre de l’ampleur, passant entre les mailles d’un filet vaccinal de plus en plus distendu.

stories.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
stories.summary: France recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures. The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. More than half of the “unprecedented” total were recorded between 25 and 27 June, with deaths of people aged ⁠75 and older accounting for about two-thirds ​of ​the excess mortality, the agency said. Visitors use umbrellas to shield themselves from the sun during a heatwave as the Arc de Triomphe in Paris at the height of the heatwave.

stories.title: Deep clean at Offaly garda station after vape incident
stories.summary: Deep clean at Offaly garda station after incident involving seized vapes Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 08:07 Edenderry Garda Station has been closed to allow for further examination A specialist deep clean is due to take place today at a Co Offaly garda station following an incident with seized vapes that resulted in two gardaí being hospitalised. Gardaí said that the two men in Edenderry Garda Station became unwell when they were involved in processing vape products. These products were seized from a commercial premises during a recent investigation. Both male members were taken to hospital, where they remain under medical assessment.

stories.title: Ryanair e l'incidente del finestrino, investigatori Usa contro la low cost: «Il ceo ha violato le regole, non esclusi problemi legati all'età o alla manutenzione del Boeing»
stories.summary: La numero uno dell’Agenzia americana per la sicurezza dei trasporti (Ntsb) striglia l’amministratore delegato di Ryanair per aver violato le norme internazionali sulla divulgazione delle informazioni relative agli incidenti aerei. E avverte che non si può ancora escludere che alla base del cedimento strutturale del motore del Boeing 737 ci siano stati «problemi legati all’età o alla manutenzione». Con una lettera di due pagine — non senza un tono polemico e con pochi precedenti — la presidente dell’Ntsb, Jennifer Homendy, scrive a Michael O’Leary, patron del colosso europeo low cost.

stories.title: Malore in volo per il comandante, atterraggio d'emergenza a Malpensa
stories.summary: Se hai scelto di non accettare i cookie di profilazione e tracciamento, puoi aderire all’abbonamento "Consentless" a un costo molto accessibile, oppure scegliere un altro abbonamento per accedere ad ANSA.it. Ti invitiamo a leggere le Condizioni Generali di Servizio , la Cookie Policy e l' Informativa Privacy .

stories.title: Ontsnapte aap uit Beekse Bergen na een maand gevangen
stories.summary: A four-year-old crowned mangabey that escaped from Safaripark Beekse Bergen in Hilvarenbeek has been recaptured after a month on the run. Caretakers employed extensive search efforts, including setting up traps and using cameras to track the monkey's movements in a 100-hectare area. The animal, which thrived in the wild due to abundant food sources, is now undergoing a medical examination. The park aims to ensure its health before determining the next steps for its return to the facility.

stories.title: India’s Modi promises fast-track courts for exam fraud fuelling protests
stories.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced the establishment of fast-track courts to address examination paper leaks, responding to widespread protests in New Delhi led by the youth-driven "Cockroach Janta Party." Demonstrators, numbering around 50,000, are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and reforms in the education system. The protests have intensified following reported irregularities in national medical school entrance exams, prompting a crackdown by security forces. Modi's pledge aims to expedite justice in a system known for its delays, but protesters remain skeptical.

stories.title: Muere el joven ganadero Luis López mientras buscaba a los toros que se le habían escapado en un pueblo de Valencia
stories.summary: Este miércoles se ha confirmado el fallecimiento de Luis López, un joven ganadero que tenía su propia cría de reses bravas en la localidad de Segorbe. El castellonense comenzó a sentirse indispuesto en pleno operativo de búsqueda de sus vacas y toros que se habían escapado en Gátova. Horas después tuvo lugar el súbito deceso. Luis decidió abrir una ganadería hace seis años y le puso su nombre. Sus animales pastaban en la finca La Laguna, de la localidad castellonense de Segorbe. Sus reses han sido exhibidas en municipios como Segorbe, Geldo, Burriana, Moncada, Caudiel, Jérica, Castellnovo, Almedíjar, La Vall d’Uixò, Ayora, Soneja, Sot de Ferrer, Benavites y, recientemente, en Gátova.

stories.title: Grootste successen vereeuwigd op nieuw Ajax-uitshirt
stories.summary: Het nieuwe Ajax-uittenue is een eerbetoon aan de rijke geschiedenis van de club, die dient als inspiratiebron voor de toekomst. Op het shirt van het zwarte tenue met oranje accenten is een lichtgrijze print te zien, waarmee negen van de grootste sportieve successen van de club zijn vastgelegd. Dit is het eerste vaste Ajax wedstrijdtenue met het adidas Originals logo, wat de klassieke uitstraling compleet maakt. Datum: 23.07.2026 Fotograaf: AFC Ajax Het tenue zal donderdag 23 juli voor het eerst op het veld te zien zijn, als Ajax in de voorronde van de UEFA Conference League aantreedt tegen FK Vojvodina.

stories.title: Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston and Kelsey Mitchell make WNBA history before All-Star Weekend
stories.summary: The 30th season of the WNBA is headed to the All-Star Break with the Indiana Fever at the top of the Eastern Conference standings and possessing the fifth-best record in the league at 17-10. Led by Caitlin Clark, who earned her second Player of the Week honor days ago and was named Player of the Month in June, the team is trying to surpass last season when they fell in the semifinals to the eventual champion Las Vegas Aces. In their final game before the break, the Fever delivered a historic performance, crushing the Connecticut Sun 123-88.

stories.title: Petition to replay Argentina vs Spain 2026 World Cup Final surpasses 61,000 signatures
stories.summary: Home > Soccer > FIFA World Cup > Argentine fans launch petition to replay the 2026 World Cup Final; signatures surpass 61,000 FIFA World Cup A campaign launched by Argentine fans is gaining momentum on a digital platform, calling for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final between Argentina and Spain to be replayed. A campaign launched by Argentine fans is gaining momentum on a digital platform, calling for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final between Argentina and Spain to be replayed. The petition, published on Change.org , has already gathered more than 61,500 signatures and urges FIFA to review what happened during the match played on July 19 in New York.
stories.trend: fans petition fifa to replay the world cup final match

stories.title: A sprint showdown on a breakaway day: How Alpecin bested Lidl-Trek on Tour stage 17
stories.summary: Tour de France A closer look at how Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France despite the best efforts of the rider in the green jersey. With three big mountain days looming and just the final day in Paris after, the big question coming into stage 17 was: sprint or breakaway. And unusually, the green jersey – worn since stage 4 by Lidl-Trek’s Mads Pedersen – came out swinging aggressively in favor of the latter. Despite looking incredibly fit throughout this year’s Tour, Pedersen had not performed well in the bunch sprint finishes, finishing seventh twice as well as ninth and 13th.

stories.title: „Wenn Leute den hohen Strompreis beklagen, sollten sie zumindest wissen, woher das kommt“
stories.summary: Die Energiewende kommt an einen kritischen Punkt: Das Stromnetz ist zu klein. Doch Elektromobilität und Wärmepumpen erfordern eine immer höhere Stromproduktion. Gleichzeitig liegen die deutschen Strompreise im europäischen Vergleich an der Spitze. Dann rückt auch noch der Kohleausstieg immer näher. Die Bundesregierung hat daher neue Gaskraftwerke ausgeschrieben und will nun den Ausbau erneuerbarer Energien stärker reglementieren. Doch eine echte Strategie für die Energiewende sei nach wie vor nicht zu erkennen, sagt Professor Volker Quaschning von der HTW Berlin im Interview mit der Ostdeutschen Allgemeinen und der Berliner Zeitung.

stories.title: Tour de France : entre Mads Pedersen et Jasper Philipsen, la lutte pour le maillot vert va se prolonger jusqu'à Paris... en passant par la montagne
stories.summary: Mads Pedersen’s grip on the Tour de France’s green jersey slipped Wednesday after Jasper Philipsen won the stage in Voiron, cutting the gap to seven points. The Belgian sprinter now leads the points classification as the race heads into the mountains. Philipsen’s victory marks the latest twist in a fierce battle for the jersey, with both riders set to fight for the title until Paris.

stories.title: EZB: Die Angst vor dem Preisschock kommt zurück
stories.summary: Sein skeptisches Gefühl hat Joachim Nagel nicht enttäuscht. Man müsse sehen, „wie belastbar das ist“, sagte der Bundesbank-Chef Mitte Juni mit Blick auf das angestrebte Friedensabkommen zwischen den USA und dem Iran. Er sollte recht behalten. Vor dem am Donnerstag anstehenden Zinsentscheid der Europäischen Zentralbank (EZB) stellt die erneute Eskalation im Irankrieg die zuletzt positiven Inflationstendenzen infrage. Dass die EZB die Zinsen im Euro-Raum vor der anstehenden Sommerpause unverändert lässt, gilt als ausgemachte Sache – und ist an den Märkten auch längst eingepreist. Die weiteren Aussichten sind durch den drastisch gestiegenen Ölpreis aber zunehmend ungewiss.

stories.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads Kaylee Hottle tributes
stories.summary: Kaylee Hottle, the 18-year-old actress known for her role in Godzilla vs Kong, tragically died in a car crash. Millie Bobby Brown and other co-stars paid tribute to Hottle, highlighting her talent and kindness. Brown expressed her devastation on Instagram, stating, "You will be deeply missed Kaylee." Hottle was set to reprise her role in the upcoming sequel, Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire, scheduled for release in 2024.

stories.title: Venice Film Festival Reveals 2026 Lineup (Updating Live)
stories.summary: The Venice Film Festival will open September 2, 2026 with Danny Boyle’s “Ink,” a biographical drama about Rupert Murdoch starring Guy Pearce, competing for the Golden Lion. The festival closes September 12 with Giovanni Veronesi’s “Dio Ride,” an out-of-competition Italian film. Netflix has acquired U.S. rights to “Ink,” while Maggie Gyllenhaal will lead the competition jury. International titles from rising directors will fill the lineup, marking a lighter Hollywood presence than recent years.

stories.title: Government to cut business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues
stories.summary: Pubs, clubs and music venues in England will get a 20% cut in business rates from April, saving firms around £1,100 each next year. The government estimates nearly 32,000 venues will benefit, costing £100m funded by reviewing tax relief for less community-focused businesses. The discount won’t apply to the largest live music venues, with eligibility details due at the autumn Budget. Hospitality leaders welcomed the move but some owners say it won’t fully offset rising costs elsewhere.

stories.title: Daniel Wu on ‘Cold War 1994,’ ‘Twilight of the Warriors’ and Coming Home to Hong Kong: ‘The Craft Still Challenges Me’
stories.summary: Daniel Wu stars in Cold War 1994, the third installment of the prequel series set before Hong Kong’s 1997 handover. The film, produced by Bill Kong and Ivy Ho, marks Wu’s return to the franchise and his home city. Wu plays Peter Choi opposite Terrance Lau’s M.B. Lee, a role previously played by Chang Kuo-chu in Cold War 2. The new movie follows the first two films, which were the highest-grossing Hong Kong productions of their respective years.

stories.title: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
stories.summary: Actor Jason Alexander has apologized to reality star Courtney Stodden for an inappropriate 2012 comedy sketch where he simulated using a phone on her chest, calling it a "mistake." The sketch, which parodied daytime TV, included lines referencing Stodden’s age at the time. Stodden, who reposted the clip this week, called it exploitative and said she lacked legal power to consent. Alexander, who co-wrote the sketch, said he was "deeply sorry" for the harm caused.

stories.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
stories.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires’ disease cluster on the Upper East Side, health officials announced, but the recent decline in new cases suggests that the source of the deadly outbreak has likely been eliminated. “It is with great sadness that I report a third person has died in connection with the Legionnaires’ disease community cluster on the Upper East Side. Our hearts are with their family and friends as they mourn their loss,” NYC Health Commissioner Dr. Alister F. Martin said Sunday. The latest death marks the third day in a row that health officials have announced the bacterial disease claimed a life.

stories.title: Andy Burnham's plan to collaborate with social media creators
stories.summary: Andy Burnham’s government plans a social-first communications strategy, prioritizing raw, creator collaborations over polished content. The push includes casual, handheld phone clips and direct policy chats on X, Instagram and TikTok to engage younger, less traditional audiences. This mirrors a trend started under the previous administration, which hosted influencer events and gave creators direct access to ministers. Expect more behind-the-scenes style posts and partnerships with online personalities in the coming months.

stories.title: Ruiz and Gavi's reward for World Cup win... 150kg of tomatoes
stories.summary: Spain midfielders Fabian Ruiz and Gavi received 150kg of tomatoes as a reward for winning the World Cup, a tradition in their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca. Ruiz, who started the final, got 85kg while Gavi received 68.5kg during an official reception on Wednesday. Former Spain winger Jesus Navas, also from the town, attended the event. The ceremony honored their achievement with a local twist tied to the municipality’s agricultural roots.

stories.title: Unbekannter stiehlt Geldbörse im Discounter – kurz darauf Konto leergeräumt
stories.summary: come-on.de Kreis und Region Stand: 23.07.2026, 09:15 Uhr Kommentare In Iserlohn kam es zu einem Taschendiebstahl in einem Discounter. (Symbolbild) © Daniel Karmann/dpa In einem Iserlohner Discounter hat ein unbekannter Mann einer 62-Jährigen die Geldbörse gestohlen. Noch am selben Tag verschwand ein vierstelliger Betrag vom Konto. Iserlohn – In einem Discounter am Wiesengrund ist einer 62-jährigen Frau die Geldbörse gestohlen worden. Kurz nach dem Diebstahl wurde ein vierstelliger Geldbetrag von ihrem Konto abgebucht. Laut Polizei Iserlohn ermittelt die Behörde wegen Diebstahls. Unbekannter nähert sich der Frau mehrfach beim Einkaufen Der Diebstahl geschah gegen 9.45 Uhr.

stories.title: Eintracht Frankfurt droht finanzstarke Konkurrenz
stories.summary: Eintracht Frankfurt faces competition for Nigerian midfielder Raphael Onyedika as Saudi Pro League club Al Ittihad reportedly tabled an official offer. The 24-year-old, under contract at FC Brügge since 2022, has 183 appearances and three trophies with the Belgian side. Frankfurt, pushing hard for a fourth summer signing, had previously been linked with a verbal agreement. The transfer saga continues as both clubs vie for the player’s signature.

stories.title: Listini europei aprono in rosso, appesantiti da Milano zavorrata da STM
stories.summary: European stock markets opened lower, dragged down by Milan’s benchmark index as chipmaker STM’s quarterly outlook fell short of expectations. STM forecasted $3.7 billion in revenue for the current quarter, up 16% year-over-year, with a gross margin around 37%. Oil prices surged over 3% to near nine-month highs, with Brent crude nearing $98 per barrel, amid rising Middle East tensions and Houthi threats in the Red Sea. Investors now await further U.S. corporate earnings reports to gauge economic momentum.

stories.title: El Gobierno propone a Yolanda Díaz como directora general de la OIT, el organismo de la ONU dedicado al trabajo
stories.summary: The Spanish government has nominated Deputy Prime Minister and Labor Minister Yolanda Díaz to lead the International Labour Organization (ILO), the UN agency overseeing global labor standards. Díaz would challenge incumbent Gilbert F. Houngbo, who has headed the ILO since 2022. The move follows criticism from Spanish unions over the agency’s direction under Houngbo’s leadership. The ILO’s next director-general will be elected in 2025.

stories.title: Charity 'heartbroken' as food ruined in break-in
stories.summary: Charity FareShare North East lost 16 tonnes of food—enough for 7,000 meals—after thieves cut power to its Middlesbrough warehouse freezers on Tuesday. The break-in ruined perishables like milk and meat, forcing disposal of spoiled supplies. The charity, which redistributes supermarket donations to over 200 groups, said the damage was heartbreaking. Staff discovered ransacked lockers and open chiller doors when arriving for work.

stories.title: Rahul Gandhi Protest LIVE: Rahul Gandhi Taken Away From Protest Site Near PM Modi's Residence
stories.summary: CNN name, logo and all associated elements ® and © 2026 Cable News Network LP, LLLP. A Time Warner Company. All rights reserved. stories.title: New Lewis Hamilton evidence as untelevised team radio emerges
stories.summary: Untelevised team radio footage from the Belgian Grand Prix has uncovered the moment Lewis Hamilton insisted that he was in “an impossible position” in his first-lap collision with Mercedes driver George Russell. It comes after PlanetF1.com revealed on Wednesday that Hamilton’s controversial five-second penalty will be raised in a meeting between drivers and the FIA ahead of this weekend’s Hungarian Grand Prix. Lewis Hamilton team radio emerges after FIA penalty over George Russell clash Want more PlanetF1.com coverage?

stories.title: Hull City transfer news: Matt Targett signed on a free
stories.summary: Hull City have signed free-agent defender Matt Targett on a two-year deal with a club option for an extra year. The 30-year-old left-back, who has Premier League experience with Southampton, Aston Villa and Newcastle, joins after his contract expired. Targett, who made 46 appearances for Middlesbrough last season, aims to guide Hull’s Championship promotion push. He will bring leadership to a side targeting an immediate top-flight return.

stories.title: Zimbabwe look to expose vulnerable India searching for winning formula
stories.summary: Big picture: Shreyas Iyer looking for first win India 's role in helping smaller cricket economies generate income via TV rights generally dominates chatter ahead of a Zimbabwe tour. But series defeats to Ireland and England have veered the discussion towards India's T20I failings and team combinations. When they arrived in Zimbabwe two years ago, they were led by Shubman Gill. Now, T20Is are the only format Gill isn't part of. The captaincy mantle has been passed on from Suryakumar Yadav to Shreyas Iyer , who is winless as captain after seven games.

stories.title: Nie żyje Irena Dudzińska. Aktorka, która wystąpiła m.in. w serialu "Świat według Kiepskich" miała 85 lat
stories.summary: Nie żyje Irena Dudzińska , polska aktorka teatralna. Grała też w filmach i serialach, m.in. w " Świecie według Kiepskich " i " Pierwszej miłości ". Informację o śmierci Ireny Dudzińskiej potwierdziła jej rodzina. Miała 85 lat. Kim była Irena Dudzińska? W latach 1988–1995 Irena Dudzińska była związana z teatrem Polskim w Poznaniu. Współpracownicy z teatru pożegnali ją w poście na Facebooku. Z głębokim smutkiem zawiadamiamy, że 18 lipca 2026 w wieku 85 lat odeszła Irena Dudzińska , polska aktorka teatralna i telewizyjna , czytamy w internetowym wpisie jej bliskich.

stories.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
stories.summary: New research from the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis reveals a stark generational divide in U.S. homeownership rates. While the traditional homeownership rate stands at 65%, a new measure shows the actual figure is closer to 53%. For adults under 35, the homeownership rate plummets to just 22%, highlighting significant disparities within the millennial generation. This trend suggests that younger millennials are struggling to achieve homeownership, while those over 35 are accumulating wealth similar to baby boomers.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 3
stories.summary: Cricket fans, test your skills: The Hundred’s daily "Who am I?" challenge features star player number three. BBC Sport’s Joe Rindl drops clues daily, with top scorers guessing correctly in three to five tries. Play now for points and return tomorrow for the next cricketer.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Alphabet, Google’s parent company, reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, the first time in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged. Revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but spending on AI is projected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. The company’s stock dropped 4% after hours following the announcement. Analysts expect continued heavy investment in servers and data centers to support AI expansion.

stories.title: HNK Rijeka tie 'a great opportunity' for Derry
stories.summary: Derry City face HNK Rijeka in the second round of the Europa Conference League qualifiers on Thursday. The tie follows Derry’s 5-3 aggregate loss to CSKA Sofia in the Europa League opener, marred by crowd trouble at the Brandywell that led to a temporary match stoppage and police intervention. As a result, Derry received a suspended partial stadium ban for two years and a €20,000 fine. Lynch’s side aims to use the experience to push for a positive result in the return leg.

stories.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
stories.summary: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

stories.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
stories.summary: River levels in Wales are nearing record lows as drought conditions intensify, with July rainfall at just 5% of average following prolonged dry spells. Natural Resources Wales is set to declare an environmental drought for parts of the country later today. The crisis has already damaged wildlife, crops, and livestock, with rivers like the Elwy in Denbighshire severely affected. Authorities warn the situation could worsen without significant rainfall.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after losing 11kg, drawing attention to exam paper leaks in India. Prime Minister Narendra Modi responded by announcing fast-track courts for those involved, calling student welfare a priority. Protesters, inspired by Wangchuk’s fast, continue demanding systemic changes to prevent leaks. The government’s measures aim to address immediate concerns while activists push for deeper reforms.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
stories.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukraine’s drone strikes on Wildberries warehouses in Russia have killed eight workers and disrupted supplies for hundreds of small businesses. Attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk destroyed logistics hubs used by sellers who rely on the online retailer, costing some up to £9,500 each. Kyiv says the strikes target military supply chains but also hit civilian livelihoods after nearly four and a half years of war. Officials report damages exceeding £750 million as operations halt at key facilities.

stories.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
stories.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has become a haven for ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper, thriving amid office blocks and a shopping centre. The 2023 project, led by Hull Butterfly City, transformed part of a Land Registry site near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre into a wildlife-friendly space. Sean Clough of the group said the small skipper’s breeding success there is unusual for an urban area. The meadow’s uncut grass provides essential shelter for caterpillars over winter.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: Animal lovers in China are escalating their campaign against animal abuse after a viral video showed the killing of a stray dog and her puppies by four boys under 14. Authorities blocked online discussion, citing protection of minors, but outrage persisted with thousands still commenting weeks later. Activists have moved protests offline and overseas, including billboards in New York. The case highlights tensions between public anger and state censorship over animal welfare.

stories.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
stories.summary: ServiceNow invested $40 million in BusinessNext, an Indian banking software firm, valuing it at $700 million. The Noida-based company, serving over 70 banks globally, will leverage ServiceNow’s sales network to expand AI-driven financial services. BusinessNext, profitable with $32 million in revenue, counts India’s central bank and top lenders among its clients. The partnership aims to accelerate growth in international markets where half its revenue is already generated.

stories.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
stories.summary: A stunning image from the International Space Station on July 23, 2026, captures the rugged beauty of Washington state’s Olympic Peninsula, showcasing snow-capped mountains, deep river valleys, and lush rainforests. The panoramic view highlights Olympic National Park’s mountainous core, surrounded by coastal waters and islands, with much of the land protected as wilderness. The mountains’ basalt rock formed 55 to 15 million years ago from undersea eruptions. Next, scientists will analyze the region’s shifting glaciers and ecosystems for climate impact insights.

stories.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
stories.summary: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

stories.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalized for respiratory infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted, compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts link the disparity to air pollution and deprivation, with absolute admissions 82% higher in poor areas. Health officials plan further analysis to identify specific causes and target interventions.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: Lego has launched a $200 Donkey Kong-themed arcade machine set featuring a functional game where Mario jumps endless barrels. Designed with input from Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto, the set includes a working joystick and jump button. Players trigger a lever to release an endless stream of classic 1989-style barrels. The set offers nostalgic play but keeps Mario from reaching the top.

stories.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Fampridine, the first drug to help some people with multiple sclerosis walk, is now available on the NHS in England. Up to 5,000 people could benefit annually. The drug boosts nerve signals, improving walking speed and distance for about 40% of users. It joins existing NHS support like physiotherapy and walking aids.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A Florida teenager dropped his lawsuit against Meta, claiming social media addiction and harm from platforms like Instagram. The 15-year-old, identified as R.K.C., had already settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. His case was set for a Los Angeles jury trial next week after Meta and YouTube previously lost a similar case. The teen’s lawyers said he sought accountability and changes to protect youth before focusing on recovery and therapy.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies needed medical attention after being taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match against Ross Smith in Blackpool on Wednesday. While leading the game 2-1, Scotland's Menzies stepped away from the oche and appeared unsteady on his feet as England's Smith and the referee guided him safely off stage. It was later confirmed that Menzies was cleared by medics backstage but was unable to return to the stage to continue, and so forfeited the match. The PDC said: "Cameron experienced high blood pressure, which caused him to feel dizzy and subsequently faint. "Following an assessment by the on-site medical team, Cameron was deemed well enough to return to his accommodation.

offbeat.title: EIntracht Frankfurt stellt neues Auswärtstrikot mit Retro-Adler vor
offbeat.summary: Die Eintracht ist retro - zumindest in Sachen Auswärtstrikot. So kehren die Frankfurter zu einem Logo zurück, das sie letztmals 1999 verwendet hatten. Und das viele Fans an eine lokale Biermarke erinnert. Schon seit einigen Tagen geisterten diverse Leaks durch die Sozialen Medien, auch gab die Frankfurter Eintracht selbst kleine Hinweise auf das, was da an diesem Donnerstag um 10 Uhr offiziell vorgestellt wurde: das neue Auswärtstrikot des hessischen Bundesligisten. Weiß mit schwarzen Sponsoren-Streifen auf der Schulter, viel mehr nicht. Kein Firlefanz, keine Spielereien und vor allem eine Replik an vergangene Tage. Denn: Der Retro-Adler ist zurück.

offbeat.title: Une épidémie de rougeole « hors de contrôle » aux Etats-Unis, sur fond de vaccino-scepticisme d’une partie des habitants et de l’administration Trump
offbeat.summary: Cet article vous est offert Pour lire gratuitement cet article réservé aux abonnés, connectez-vous Se connecter Vous n'êtes pas inscrit sur Le Monde ? Inscrivez-vous gratuitement Planète Planète Planète Santé mondiale Santé mondiale Santé mondiale Le pays fait face à une accélération du nombre de cas de cette maladie aux conséquences sanitaires potentiellement graves. La baisse de la couverture vaccinale est la principale cause de cette crise. Article réservé aux abonnés Les Etats-Unis font face à une épidémie de rougeole qui ne cesse de prendre de l’ampleur, passant entre les mailles d’un filet vaccinal de plus en plus distendu.

offbeat.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
offbeat.summary: France recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures. The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. More than half of the “unprecedented” total were recorded between 25 and 27 June, with deaths of people aged ⁠75 and older accounting for about two-thirds ​of ​the excess mortality, the agency said. Visitors use umbrellas to shield themselves from the sun during a heatwave as the Arc de Triomphe in Paris at the height of the heatwave.

offbeat.title: Deep clean at Offaly garda station after vape incident
offbeat.summary: Deep clean at Offaly garda station after incident involving seized vapes Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 08:07 Edenderry Garda Station has been closed to allow for further examination A specialist deep clean is due to take place today at a Co Offaly garda station following an incident with seized vapes that resulted in two gardaí being hospitalised. Gardaí said that the two men in Edenderry Garda Station became unwell when they were involved in processing vape products. These products were seized from a commercial premises during a recent investigation. Both male members were taken to hospital, where they remain under medical assessment.

offbeat.title: Ryanair e l'incidente del finestrino, investigatori Usa contro la low cost: «Il ceo ha violato le regole, non esclusi problemi legati all'età o alla manutenzione del Boeing»
offbeat.summary: La numero uno dell’Agenzia americana per la sicurezza dei trasporti (Ntsb) striglia l’amministratore delegato di Ryanair per aver violato le norme internazionali sulla divulgazione delle informazioni relative agli incidenti aerei. E avverte che non si può ancora escludere che alla base del cedimento strutturale del motore del Boeing 737 ci siano stati «problemi legati all’età o alla manutenzione». Con una lettera di due pagine — non senza un tono polemico e con pochi precedenti — la presidente dell’Ntsb, Jennifer Homendy, scrive a Michael O’Leary, patron del colosso europeo low cost.

offbeat.title: Malore in volo per il comandante, atterraggio d'emergenza a Malpensa
offbeat.summary: Se hai scelto di non accettare i cookie di profilazione e tracciamento, puoi aderire all’abbonamento "Consentless" a un costo molto accessibile, oppure scegliere un altro abbonamento per accedere ad ANSA.it. Ti invitiamo a leggere le Condizioni Generali di Servizio , la Cookie Policy e l' Informativa Privacy .

offbeat.title: Ontsnapte aap uit Beekse Bergen na een maand gevangen
offbeat.summary: A four-year-old crowned mangabey that escaped from Safaripark Beekse Bergen in Hilvarenbeek has been recaptured after a month on the run. Caretakers employed extensive search efforts, including setting up traps and using cameras to track the monkey's movements in a 100-hectare area. The animal, which thrived in the wild due to abundant food sources, is now undergoing a medical examination. The park aims to ensure its health before determining the next steps for its return to the facility.

offbeat.title: India’s Modi promises fast-track courts for exam fraud fuelling protests
offbeat.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced the establishment of fast-track courts to address examination paper leaks, responding to widespread protests in New Delhi led by the youth-driven "Cockroach Janta Party." Demonstrators, numbering around 50,000, are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and reforms in the education system. The protests have intensified following reported irregularities in national medical school entrance exams, prompting a crackdown by security forces. Modi's pledge aims to expedite justice in a system known for its delays, but protesters remain skeptical.

offbeat.title: Muere el joven ganadero Luis López mientras buscaba a los toros que se le habían escapado en un pueblo de Valencia
offbeat.summary: Este miércoles se ha confirmado el fallecimiento de Luis López, un joven ganadero que tenía su propia cría de reses bravas en la localidad de Segorbe. El castellonense comenzó a sentirse indispuesto en pleno operativo de búsqueda de sus vacas y toros que se habían escapado en Gátova. Horas después tuvo lugar el súbito deceso. Luis decidió abrir una ganadería hace seis años y le puso su nombre. Sus animales pastaban en la finca La Laguna, de la localidad castellonense de Segorbe. Sus reses han sido exhibidas en municipios como Segorbe, Geldo, Burriana, Moncada, Caudiel, Jérica, Castellnovo, Almedíjar, La Vall d’Uixò, Ayora, Soneja, Sot de Ferrer, Benavites y, recientemente, en Gátova.

offbeat.title: Grootste successen vereeuwigd op nieuw Ajax-uitshirt
offbeat.summary: Het nieuwe Ajax-uittenue is een eerbetoon aan de rijke geschiedenis van de club, die dient als inspiratiebron voor de toekomst. Op het shirt van het zwarte tenue met oranje accenten is een lichtgrijze print te zien, waarmee negen van de grootste sportieve successen van de club zijn vastgelegd. Dit is het eerste vaste Ajax wedstrijdtenue met het adidas Originals logo, wat de klassieke uitstraling compleet maakt. Datum: 23.07.2026 Fotograaf: AFC Ajax Het tenue zal donderdag 23 juli voor het eerst op het veld te zien zijn, als Ajax in de voorronde van de UEFA Conference League aantreedt tegen FK Vojvodina.

offbeat.title: Caitlin Clark, Aliyah Boston and Kelsey Mitchell make WNBA history before All-Star Weekend
offbeat.summary: The 30th season of the WNBA is headed to the All-Star Break with the Indiana Fever at the top of the Eastern Conference standings and possessing the fifth-best record in the league at 17-10. Led by Caitlin Clark, who earned her second Player of the Week honor days ago and was named Player of the Month in June, the team is trying to surpass last season when they fell in the semifinals to the eventual champion Las Vegas Aces. In their final game before the break, the Fever delivered a historic performance, crushing the Connecticut Sun 123-88.

offbeat.title: Petition to replay Argentina vs Spain 2026 World Cup Final surpasses 61,000 signatures
offbeat.summary: Home > Soccer > FIFA World Cup > Argentine fans launch petition to replay the 2026 World Cup Final; signatures surpass 61,000 FIFA World Cup A campaign launched by Argentine fans is gaining momentum on a digital platform, calling for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final between Argentina and Spain to be replayed. A campaign launched by Argentine fans is gaining momentum on a digital platform, calling for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final between Argentina and Spain to be replayed. The petition, published on Change.org , has already gathered more than 61,500 signatures and urges FIFA to review what happened during the match played on July 19 in New York.
offbeat.trend: fans petition fifa to replay the world cup final match

offbeat.title: A sprint showdown on a breakaway day: How Alpecin bested Lidl-Trek on Tour stage 17
offbeat.summary: Tour de France A closer look at how Jasper Philipsen won stage 17 of the Tour de France despite the best efforts of the rider in the green jersey. With three big mountain days looming and just the final day in Paris after, the big question coming into stage 17 was: sprint or breakaway. And unusually, the green jersey – worn since stage 4 by Lidl-Trek’s Mads Pedersen – came out swinging aggressively in favor of the latter. Despite looking incredibly fit throughout this year’s Tour, Pedersen had not performed well in the bunch sprint finishes, finishing seventh twice as well as ninth and 13th.

offbeat.title: „Wenn Leute den hohen Strompreis beklagen, sollten sie zumindest wissen, woher das kommt“
offbeat.summary: Die Energiewende kommt an einen kritischen Punkt: Das Stromnetz ist zu klein. Doch Elektromobilität und Wärmepumpen erfordern eine immer höhere Stromproduktion. Gleichzeitig liegen die deutschen Strompreise im europäischen Vergleich an der Spitze. Dann rückt auch noch der Kohleausstieg immer näher. Die Bundesregierung hat daher neue Gaskraftwerke ausgeschrieben und will nun den Ausbau erneuerbarer Energien stärker reglementieren. Doch eine echte Strategie für die Energiewende sei nach wie vor nicht zu erkennen, sagt Professor Volker Quaschning von der HTW Berlin im Interview mit der Ostdeutschen Allgemeinen und der Berliner Zeitung.

offbeat.title: Tour de France : entre Mads Pedersen et Jasper Philipsen, la lutte pour le maillot vert va se prolonger jusqu'à Paris... en passant par la montagne
offbeat.summary: Mads Pedersen’s grip on the Tour de France’s green jersey slipped Wednesday after Jasper Philipsen won the stage in Voiron, cutting the gap to seven points. The Belgian sprinter now leads the points classification as the race heads into the mountains. Philipsen’s victory marks the latest twist in a fierce battle for the jersey, with both riders set to fight for the title until Paris.

offbeat.title: EZB: Die Angst vor dem Preisschock kommt zurück
offbeat.summary: Sein skeptisches Gefühl hat Joachim Nagel nicht enttäuscht. Man müsse sehen, „wie belastbar das ist“, sagte der Bundesbank-Chef Mitte Juni mit Blick auf das angestrebte Friedensabkommen zwischen den USA und dem Iran. Er sollte recht behalten. Vor dem am Donnerstag anstehenden Zinsentscheid der Europäischen Zentralbank (EZB) stellt die erneute Eskalation im Irankrieg die zuletzt positiven Inflationstendenzen infrage. Dass die EZB die Zinsen im Euro-Raum vor der anstehenden Sommerpause unverändert lässt, gilt als ausgemachte Sache – und ist an den Märkten auch längst eingepreist. Die weiteren Aussichten sind durch den drastisch gestiegenen Ölpreis aber zunehmend ungewiss.

offbeat.title: 'Devastated' Millie Bobby Brown leads Kaylee Hottle tributes
offbeat.summary: Kaylee Hottle, the 18-year-old actress known for her role in Godzilla vs Kong, tragically died in a car crash. Millie Bobby Brown and other co-stars paid tribute to Hottle, highlighting her talent and kindness. Brown expressed her devastation on Instagram, stating, "You will be deeply missed Kaylee." Hottle was set to reprise her role in the upcoming sequel, Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire, scheduled for release in 2024.

offbeat.title: Third person dies from NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak
offbeat.summary: A third person has died from the Legionnaires’ disease cluster on the Upper East Side, health officials announced, but the recent decline in new cases suggests that the source of the deadly outbreak has likely been eliminated. “It is with great sadness that I report a third person has died in connection with the Legionnaires’ disease community cluster on the Upper East Side. Our hearts are with their family and friends as they mourn their loss,” NYC Health Commissioner Dr. Alister F. Martin said Sunday. The latest death marks the third day in a row that health officials have announced the bacterial disease claimed a life.

offbeat.title: Unbekannter stiehlt Geldbörse im Discounter – kurz darauf Konto leergeräumt
offbeat.summary: come-on.de Kreis und Region Stand: 23.07.2026, 09:15 Uhr Kommentare In Iserlohn kam es zu einem Taschendiebstahl in einem Discounter. (Symbolbild) © Daniel Karmann/dpa In einem Iserlohner Discounter hat ein unbekannter Mann einer 62-Jährigen die Geldbörse gestohlen. Noch am selben Tag verschwand ein vierstelliger Betrag vom Konto. Iserlohn – In einem Discounter am Wiesengrund ist einer 62-jährigen Frau die Geldbörse gestohlen worden. Kurz nach dem Diebstahl wurde ein vierstelliger Geldbetrag von ihrem Konto abgebucht. Laut Polizei Iserlohn ermittelt die Behörde wegen Diebstahls. Unbekannter nähert sich der Frau mehrfach beim Einkaufen Der Diebstahl geschah gegen 9.45 Uhr.

offbeat.title: Eintracht Frankfurt droht finanzstarke Konkurrenz
offbeat.summary: Eintracht Frankfurt faces competition for Nigerian midfielder Raphael Onyedika as Saudi Pro League club Al Ittihad reportedly tabled an official offer. The 24-year-old, under contract at FC Brügge since 2022, has 183 appearances and three trophies with the Belgian side. Frankfurt, pushing hard for a fourth summer signing, had previously been linked with a verbal agreement. The transfer saga continues as both clubs vie for the player’s signature.

offbeat.title: Listini europei aprono in rosso, appesantiti da Milano zavorrata da STM
offbeat.summary: European stock markets opened lower, dragged down by Milan’s benchmark index as chipmaker STM’s quarterly outlook fell short of expectations. STM forecasted $3.7 billion in revenue for the current quarter, up 16% year-over-year, with a gross margin around 37%. Oil prices surged over 3% to near nine-month highs, with Brent crude nearing $98 per barrel, amid rising Middle East tensions and Houthi threats in the Red Sea. Investors now await further U.S. corporate earnings reports to gauge economic momentum.

offbeat.title: El Gobierno propone a Yolanda Díaz como directora general de la OIT, el organismo de la ONU dedicado al trabajo
offbeat.summary: The Spanish government has nominated Deputy Prime Minister and Labor Minister Yolanda Díaz to lead the International Labour Organization (ILO), the UN agency overseeing global labor standards. Díaz would challenge incumbent Gilbert F. Houngbo, who has headed the ILO since 2022. The move follows criticism from Spanish unions over the agency’s direction under Houngbo’s leadership. The ILO’s next director-general will be elected in 2025.

offbeat.title: Rahul Gandhi Protest LIVE: Rahul Gandhi Taken Away From Protest Site Near PM Modi's Residence
offbeat.summary: CNN name, logo and all associated elements ® and © 2026 Cable News Network LP, LLLP. A Time Warner Company. All rights reserved. CNN and the CNN logo are registered marks of Cable News Network, LP LLLP, displayed with permission. Use of the CNN name and/or logo on or as part of NEWS18.com does not derogate from the intellectual property rights of Cable News Network in respect of them. © Copyright Network18 Media and Investments Ltd 2026.

offbeat.title: New Lewis Hamilton evidence as untelevised team radio emerges
offbeat.summary: Untelevised team radio footage from the Belgian Grand Prix has uncovered the moment Lewis Hamilton insisted that he was in “an impossible position” in his first-lap collision with Mercedes driver George Russell. It comes after PlanetF1.com revealed on Wednesday that Hamilton’s controversial five-second penalty will be raised in a meeting between drivers and the FIA ahead of this weekend’s Hungarian Grand Prix. Lewis Hamilton team radio emerges after FIA penalty over George Russell clash Want more PlanetF1.com coverage?

offbeat.title: Hull City transfer news: Matt Targett signed on a free
offbeat.summary: Hull City have signed free-agent defender Matt Targett on a two-year deal with a club option for an extra year. The 30-year-old left-back, who has Premier League experience with Southampton, Aston Villa and Newcastle, joins after his contract expired. Targett, who made 46 appearances for Middlesbrough last season, aims to guide Hull’s Championship promotion push. He will bring leadership to a side targeting an immediate top-flight return.

offbeat.title: Zimbabwe look to expose vulnerable India searching for winning formula
offbeat.summary: Big picture: Shreyas Iyer looking for first win India 's role in helping smaller cricket economies generate income via TV rights generally dominates chatter ahead of a Zimbabwe tour. But series defeats to Ireland and England have veered the discussion towards India's T20I failings and team combinations. When they arrived in Zimbabwe two years ago, they were led by Shubman Gill. Now, T20Is are the only format Gill isn't part of. The captaincy mantle has been passed on from Suryakumar Yadav to Shreyas Iyer , who is winless as captain after seven games.

offbeat.title: Nie żyje Irena Dudzińska. Aktorka, która wystąpiła m.in. w serialu "Świat według Kiepskich" miała 85 lat
offbeat.summary: Nie żyje Irena Dudzińska , polska aktorka teatralna. Grała też w filmach i serialach, m.in. w " Świecie według Kiepskich " i " Pierwszej miłości ". Informację o śmierci Ireny Dudzińskiej potwierdziła jej rodzina. Miała 85 lat. Kim była Irena Dudzińska? W latach 1988–1995 Irena Dudzińska była związana z teatrem Polskim w Poznaniu. Współpracownicy z teatru pożegnali ją w poście na Facebooku. Z głębokim smutkiem zawiadamiamy, że 18 lipca 2026 w wieku 85 lat odeszła Irena Dudzińska , polska aktorka teatralna i telewizyjna , czytamy w internetowym wpisie jej bliskich.

offbeat.title: The millennial generation has split, new Fed research shows: those over 35 are edging toward boomer-style wealth, while everyone else falls behind
offbeat.summary: New research from the Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis reveals a stark generational divide in U.S. homeownership rates. While the traditional homeownership rate stands at 65%, a new measure shows the actual figure is closer to 53%. For adults under 35, the homeownership rate plummets to just 22%, highlighting significant disparities within the millennial generation. This trend suggests that younger millennials are struggling to achieve homeownership, while those over 35 are accumulating wealth similar to baby boomers.

offbeat.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
offbeat.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike after losing 11kg, drawing attention to exam paper leaks in India. Prime Minister Narendra Modi responded by announcing fast-track courts for those involved, calling student welfare a priority. Protesters, inspired by Wangchuk’s fast, continue demanding systemic changes to prevent leaks. The government’s measures aim to address immediate concerns while activists push for deeper reforms.

offbeat.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
offbeat.summary: Ukraine’s drone strikes on Wildberries warehouses in Russia have killed eight workers and disrupted supplies for hundreds of small businesses. Attacks in Elektrostal and Kotovsk destroyed logistics hubs used by sellers who rely on the online retailer, costing some up to £9,500 each. Kyiv says the strikes target military supply chains but also hit civilian livelihoods after nearly four and a half years of war. Officials report damages exceeding £750 million as operations halt at key facilities.

offbeat.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
offbeat.summary: Animal lovers in China are escalating their campaign against animal abuse after a viral video showed the killing of a stray dog and her puppies by four boys under 14. Authorities blocked online discussion, citing protection of minors, but outrage persisted with thousands still commenting weeks later. Activists have moved protests offline and overseas, including billboards in New York. The case highlights tensions between public anger and state censorship over animal welfare.

entertainment.title: Venice Film Festival Reveals 2026 Lineup (Updating Live)
entertainment.summary: The Venice Film Festival will open September 2, 2026 with Danny Boyle’s “Ink,” a biographical drama about Rupert Murdoch starring Guy Pearce, competing for the Golden Lion. The festival closes September 12 with Giovanni Veronesi’s “Dio Ride,” an out-of-competition Italian film. Netflix has acquired U.S. rights to “Ink,” while Maggie Gyllenhaal will lead the competition jury. International titles from rising directors will fill the lineup, marking a lighter Hollywood presence than recent years.

entertainment.title: Daniel Wu on ‘Cold War 1994,’ ‘Twilight of the Warriors’ and Coming Home to Hong Kong: ‘The Craft Still Challenges Me’
entertainment.summary: Daniel Wu stars in Cold War 1994, the third installment of the prequel series set before Hong Kong’s 1997 handover. The film, produced by Bill Kong and Ivy Ho, marks Wu’s return to the franchise and his home city. Wu plays Peter Choi opposite Terrance Lau’s M.B. Lee, a role previously played by Chang Kuo-chu in Cold War 2. The new movie follows the first two films, which were the highest-grossing Hong Kong productions of their respective years.

entertainment.title: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
entertainment.summary: Actor Jason Alexander has apologized to reality star Courtney Stodden for an inappropriate 2012 comedy sketch where he simulated using a phone on her chest, calling it a "mistake." The sketch, which parodied daytime TV, included lines referencing Stodden’s age at the time. Stodden, who reposted the clip this week, called it exploitative and said she lacked legal power to consent. Alexander, who co-wrote the sketch, said he was "deeply sorry" for the harm caused.

crisis.title: Government to cut business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues
crisis.summary: Pubs, clubs and music venues in England will get a 20% cut in business rates from April, saving firms around £1,100 each next year. The government estimates nearly 32,000 venues will benefit, costing £100m funded by reviewing tax relief for less community-focused businesses. The discount won’t apply to the largest live music venues, with eligibility details due at the autumn Budget. Hospitality leaders welcomed the move but some owners say it won’t fully offset rising costs elsewhere.

crisis.title: Charity 'heartbroken' as food ruined in break-in
crisis.summary: Charity FareShare North East lost 16 tonnes of food—enough for 7,000 meals—after thieves cut power to its Middlesbrough warehouse freezers on Tuesday. The break-in ruined perishables like milk and meat, forcing disposal of spoiled supplies. The charity, which redistributes supermarket donations to over 200 groups, said the damage was heartbreaking. Staff discovered ransacked lockers and open chiller doors when arriving for work.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
crisis.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

politics.title: Andy Burnham's plan to collaborate with social media creators
politics.summary: Andy Burnham’s government plans a social-first communications strategy, prioritizing raw, creator collaborations over polished content. The push includes casual, handheld phone clips and direct policy chats on X, Instagram and TikTok to engage younger, less traditional audiences. This mirrors a trend started under the previous administration, which hosted influencer events and gave creators direct access to ministers. Expect more behind-the-scenes style posts and partnerships with online personalities in the coming months.

politics.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
politics.summary: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

politics.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
politics.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

sports.title: Ruiz and Gavi's reward for World Cup win... 150kg of tomatoes
sports.summary: Spain midfielders Fabian Ruiz and Gavi received 150kg of tomatoes as a reward for winning the World Cup, a tradition in their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca. Ruiz, who started the final, got 85kg while Gavi received 68.5kg during an official reception on Wednesday. Former Spain winger Jesus Navas, also from the town, attended the event. The ceremony honored their achievement with a local twist tied to the municipality’s agricultural roots.

sports.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 3
sports.summary: Cricket fans, test your skills: The Hundred’s daily "Who am I?" challenge features star player number three. BBC Sport’s Joe Rindl drops clues daily, with top scorers guessing correctly in three to five tries. Play now for points and return tomorrow for the next cricketer.

sports.title: HNK Rijeka tie 'a great opportunity' for Derry
sports.summary: Derry City face HNK Rijeka in the second round of the Europa Conference League qualifiers on Thursday. The tie follows Derry’s 5-3 aggregate loss to CSKA Sofia in the Europa League opener, marred by crowd trouble at the Brandywell that led to a temporary match stoppage and police intervention. As a result, Derry received a suspended partial stadium ban for two years and a €20,000 fine. Lynch’s side aims to use the experience to push for a positive result in the return leg.

breaking.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
breaking.summary: Alphabet, Google’s parent company, reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, the first time in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged. Revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but spending on AI is projected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. The company’s stock dropped 4% after hours following the announcement. Analysts expect continued heavy investment in servers and data centers to support AI expansion.

breaking.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
breaking.summary: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

breaking.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
breaking.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

facts.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
facts.summary: River levels in Wales are nearing record lows as drought conditions intensify, with July rainfall at just 5% of average following prolonged dry spells. Natural Resources Wales is set to declare an environmental drought for parts of the country later today. The crisis has already damaged wildlife, crops, and livestock, with rivers like the Elwy in Denbighshire severely affected. Authorities warn the situation could worsen without significant rainfall.

facts.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
facts.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has become a haven for ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper, thriving amid office blocks and a shopping centre. The 2023 project, led by Hull Butterfly City, transformed part of a Land Registry site near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre into a wildlife-friendly space. Sean Clough of the group said the small skipper’s breeding success there is unusual for an urban area. The meadow’s uncut grass provides essential shelter for caterpillars over winter.

facts.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
facts.summary: A stunning image from the International Space Station on July 23, 2026, captures the rugged beauty of Washington state’s Olympic Peninsula, showcasing snow-capped mountains, deep river valleys, and lush rainforests. The panoramic view highlights Olympic National Park’s mountainous core, surrounded by coastal waters and islands, with much of the land protected as wilderness. The mountains’ basalt rock formed 55 to 15 million years ago from undersea eruptions. Next, scientists will analyze the region’s shifting glaciers and ecosystems for climate impact insights.

tech.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
tech.summary: ServiceNow invested $40 million in BusinessNext, an Indian banking software firm, valuing it at $700 million. The Noida-based company, serving over 70 banks globally, will leverage ServiceNow’s sales network to expand AI-driven financial services. BusinessNext, profitable with $32 million in revenue, counts India’s central bank and top lenders among its clients. The partnership aims to accelerate growth in international markets where half its revenue is already generated.

tech.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
tech.summary: Lego has launched a $200 Donkey Kong-themed arcade machine set featuring a functional game where Mario jumps endless barrels. Designed with input from Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto, the set includes a working joystick and jump button. Players trigger a lever to release an endless stream of classic 1989-style barrels. The set offers nostalgic play but keeps Mario from reaching the top.

tech.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
tech.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

health.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
health.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalized for respiratory infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024/25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted, compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts link the disparity to air pollution and deprivation, with absolute admissions 82% higher in poor areas. Health officials plan further analysis to identify specific causes and target interventions.

health.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
health.summary: Fampridine, the first drug to help some people with multiple sclerosis walk, is now available on the NHS in England. Up to 5,000 people could benefit annually. The drug boosts nerve signals, improving walking speed and distance for about 40% of users. It joins existing NHS support like physiotherapy and walking aids.

health.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
health.summary: A Florida teenager dropped his lawsuit against Meta, claiming social media addiction and harm from platforms like Instagram. The 15-year-old, identified as R.K.C., had already settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. His case was set for a Los Angeles jury trial next week after Meta and YouTube previously lost a similar case. The teen’s lawyers said he sought accountability and changes to protect youth before focusing on recovery and therapy.
